message:
br-bd-bivg2 wire renameat2 RENAME_NOREPLACE end-to-end through FsOps

FUSE_RENAME2 (opcode 45) carries a flags bitset; pre-fix the
dispatcher rejected every non-zero flag with EINVAL, so user code
issuing renameat2(..., RENAME_NOREPLACE) over the FUSE mount could
not get the atomic-no-overwrite semantics it asked for — the
overwrite-then-detect pattern is unsafe under concurrent create.

Changes:
- vfs::FsOps::rename2(flags) added with a default impl that delegates
  to rename() for flags=0 and returns EINVAL for any non-zero flags.
  Existing FsOps stubs (StubFs, IoctlRecordingFs, CountingReadFs,
  HookFs, MutationRecordingFs, RenameConcurrencyProbeFs, NeverCalledFs,
  Ext4FsOps) inherit the default — no per-stub edits needed.
- OpenFs overrides rename2 to honour RENAME_NOREPLACE: under the
  parent + new_parent FuseInodeLocks already held by the dispatcher
  it does a lookup of (new_parent, new_name) and returns EEXIST if
  resolved, otherwise falls through to the classic rename. Unknown
  flag bits, RENAME_NOREPLACE | RENAME_EXCHANGE, RENAME_EXCHANGE,
  and RENAME_WHITEOUT all return EINVAL pending the dual-entry /
  whiteout-inode work tracked in bd-5lxot.
- ffs-fuse::dispatch_rename takes flags: u32 and forwards through
  ops.rename2(...). The pre-fix blanket EINVAL guard in the FUSE
  rename callback is removed. All in-tree dispatch_rename callers
  (1 production + 4 tests) updated to pass flags=0.

Tests: 642 ffs-core --lib + 250 ffs-fuse --lib pass. Six new
ffs-core regressions: classic flag=0 still overwrites, NOREPLACE
over existing target returns EEXIST and leaves both names intact,
NOREPLACE with free target succeeds and preserves the renamed
inode, EXCHANGE returns EINVAL, NOREPLACE+EXCHANGE returns EINVAL,
and unknown flag bits return EINVAL. Clippy + fmt clean via
rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_bivg2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -669,6 +669,39 @@ pub trait FsOps: Send + Sync {
         Err(FfsError::ReadOnly)
     }
 
+    /// Rename with `renameat2(2)` flags (FUSE_RENAME2 opcode).
+    ///
+    /// `flags` is the bitset defined by `<linux/fs.h>`:
+    /// - `0`  — classic rename (overwrite target if it exists).
+    /// - `RENAME_NOREPLACE` (1) — fail with `EEXIST` if the destination
+    ///   exists. The check + rename must be atomic under the parent
+    ///   directory locks held by the caller.
+    /// - `RENAME_EXCHANGE` (2) — atomically swap two existing entries.
+    /// - `RENAME_WHITEOUT` (4) — used by overlayfs; not currently supported.
+    ///
+    /// The default implementation delegates to [`Self::rename`] when
+    /// `flags == 0` and returns `EINVAL` for any non-zero flags so that
+    /// callers cannot silently get classic-rename semantics on an
+    /// implementation that does not honour the requested mode.
+    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
+    fn rename2(
+        &self,
+        cx: &Cx,
+        scope: &mut RequestScope,
+        parent: InodeNumber,
+        name: &OsStr,
+        new_parent: InodeNumber,
+        new_name: &OsStr,
+        flags: u32,
+    ) -> ffs_error::Result<()> {
+        if flags == 0 {
+            return self.rename(cx, scope, parent, name, new_parent, new_name);
+        }
+        Err(FfsError::Io(std::io::Error::from_raw_os_error(
+            libc::EINVAL,
+        )))
+    }
+
     /// Write data to file `ino` at byte `offset`. Returns bytes written.
     fn write(
         &self,
